Workflows.title: Production Deployment Workflow
Workflows.description: The primary NTWF ABox example. A 5-step workflow for deploying software to production at Special Solutions. Steps involve an AI Risk Analysis Agent (step 1), a human Legal Compliance Reviewer (step 2), a human Release Manager at the Release Approval Gate (step 3), an automated CI/CD pipeline (step 4), and the Release Manager again for the post-deployment report (step 5). Demonstrates all three agent types, the delegation chain, artifact provenance, and DCAT dataset consumption.

Roles.label: Legal Compliance Reviewer
Roles.comment: Certifies that the deployment plan meets regulatory and contractual requirements. Must be a human agent — AI agents may not fill this role (requiresHumanApproval).

Roles.label: CI/CD Executor
Roles.comment: Executes the automated build, test, and deployment sequence. Filled by an AutomatedPipeline. Demonstrates CQ3: this step does NOT require human approval.

Roles.label: Chief Technology Officer
Roles.comment: Top of the delegation chain. Has cap-executive-authorization. When both Release Manager and VP Engineering are unavailable, the CTO is the final escalation target.

Roles.label: Risk Analysis Agent
Roles.comment: Performs probabilistic risk scoring over the Q1 risk metrics dataset. This is a role, not an identity — filled by RiskAnalysis-AI today but could be replaced by a newer model version without changing the workflow structure.

Roles.label: Deployment Health Agent
Roles.comment: Generates the post-deployment health report by summarizing telemetry after a release. A role, not an identity — filled by DeploymentHealth-AI today (a probabilistic summarizer), it could be replaced by a newer model without changing the workflow structure. This is the second AI-executed step the article's CQ3 counts (two steps executed by AI agents).

Roles.label: Release Manager
Roles.comment: Owns the production deployment process. Coordinates all parties, triggers the pipeline, and authorizes the release at the approval gate.

Roles.label: VP of Engineering
Roles.comment: First escalation target when Release Manager is unavailable. Demonstrates ntwf:delegatesTo chain: Release Manager → VP Engineering → CTO.

AutomatedPipelines.name: CI/CD Deploy Pipeline
AutomatedPipelines.description: Automated build, test, and deployment pipeline for production releases. Deterministic execution — no probabilistic output.

WorkflowStatusConcepts.definition: Workflow is being authored or reviewed and is not yet in production use.
WorkflowStatusConcepts.scopeNote: Set this status when the workflow has not completed its initial review cycle.

WorkflowStatusConcepts.definition: Workflow is approved and currently being executed by the organization.
WorkflowStatusConcepts.scopeNote: Only active workflows should be assigned to running process instances.

WorkflowStatusConcepts.definition: Workflow has been replaced by a newer version and should not be used for new instances.
WorkflowStatusConcepts.scopeNote: Deprecated workflows may still have in-flight instances; do not archive until all instances close.

WorkflowStatusConcepts.definition: Workflow is no longer in use and has been moved to historical records.
WorkflowStatusConcepts.scopeNote: Archived workflows answer CQ5 stale-workflow queries but generate no new instances.

ComplianceVerdictConcepts.prefLabel: AT RISK: stale workflow with an AI-executed step
ComplianceVerdictConcepts.definition: The workflow is BOTH stale (compliance documentation not reviewed in twelve months) AND has at least one step executed by an AI agent — the highest-compliance-risk combination the article identifies.
ComplianceVerdictConcepts.scopeNote: Resolved when IsAtComplianceRisk is true. This is the verdict Talisman's closing query returns.

ComplianceVerdictConcepts.prefLabel: OK: not a stale-plus-AI compliance risk
ComplianceVerdictConcepts.definition: The workflow does not meet the stale-AND-AI-executed combination. It may still be stale, or still have AI steps, but not both at once.
ComplianceVerdictConcepts.scopeNote: Resolved when IsAtComplianceRisk is false. The default, non-risk outcome.

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must be a human agent capable of contextual, ethical, and legally binding decisions.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Used for ApprovalGate steps where requiresHumanApproval is true. AI agents may not fill roles with this capability.

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must be capable of probabilistic risk scoring over structured datasets.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Can be satisfied by an AIAgent with an appropriate model version (e.g. risk-classifier-v2.4.1).

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must have authority to certify regulatory and contractual compliance.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Requires a human agent. Cannot be delegated to AI or automated pipeline.

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must be an automated pipeline capable of running a deterministic build, test, and deploy sequence.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Satisfied by AutomatedPipelines. HumanAgents should not fill roles requiring only this capability.

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must hold executive authority to approve high-impact organizational changes.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Reserved for the top of delegation chains. Maps to the ntwf:delegatesTo escalation pattern.
